# Triton Exercise Bank

15 exercise seeds covering the 6 Triton topics from `triton-tutor-setup`. Each
entry MUST have all 10 required fields (validated by
`scripts/validate-exercise-bank.py`).

Topic slugs: `triton-basics`, `tiling-autotuning`, `matmul-patterns`,
`attention-reductions`, `compiler-internals`, `ecosystem-production`.

Difficulty notation: ★☆☆ Easy, ★★☆ Medium, ★★★ Hard.

---

### E-TRI-01 Vector Add
- Topic: triton-basics
- Difficulty: ★☆☆
- Concepts exercised: `@triton.jit`, `tl.program_id`, `tl.arange`, masked `tl.load`/`tl.store`, BLOCK_SIZE constexpr
- Prerequisites: none
- Reference: Triton tutorial 01-vector-add.py, OpenAI Triton paper §3
- Estimated learner time: 1–2 hours
- Performance target: ≥ 95% of `torch.add` throughput on N=2^24 FP32 on H100
- Test sizes: 1, 1024, 65536, 4194304
- atol/rtol: 1e-5 / 1e-5
- FIXME breakdown: 1. compute pid offsets; 2. build mask; 3. masked load A,B; 4. add and masked store

### E-TRI-02 Fused Softmax
- Topic: triton-basics
- Difficulty: ★★☆
- Concepts exercised: per-row softmax kernel, fused max-subtract, numerically stable `tl.exp`, row stride
- Prerequisites: E-TRI-01
- Reference: Triton tutorial 02-fused-softmax.py
- Estimated learner time: 2–4 hours
- Performance target: ≥ 2× speedup over `torch.softmax` for row width 1024, batch 1024, FP32 on H100
- Test sizes: 128x128, 1024x512, 1024x4096, 2048x16384
- atol/rtol: 1e-5 / 1e-5
- FIXME breakdown: 1. load row chunk with column mask; 2. compute row max; 3. exp(x - max); 4. sum and divide

### E-TRI-03 Block Matmul
- Topic: matmul-patterns
- Difficulty: ★★☆
- Concepts exercised: tiled GEMM, `tl.dot`, accumulator dtype, BLOCK_M/BLOCK_N/BLOCK_K, K-loop
- Prerequisites: E-TRI-01
- Reference: Triton tutorial 03-matrix-multiplication.py
- Estimated learner time: 4–6 hours
- Performance target: ≥ 80% of `torch.matmul` on 4096x4096 FP16 on H100
- Test sizes: 64, 512, 1024, 4096
- atol/rtol: 1e-2 / 1e-3
- FIXME breakdown: 1. compute block indices; 2. allocate accumulator; 3. K-loop with tl.dot; 4. cast acc to output dtype; 5. masked store

### E-TRI-04 Autotune Sweep
- Topic: tiling-autotuning
- Difficulty: ★★☆
- Concepts exercised: `triton.autotune`, `triton.Config`, `num_warps`, `num_stages`, `key=[...]` cache, pruning
- Prerequisites: E-TRI-03
- Reference: Triton tutorial 03-matrix-multiplication.py L60–L100, Triton API `triton.autotune`
- Estimated learner time: 3–5 hours
- Performance target: ≥ 10% speedup vs the un-autotuned E-TRI-03 baseline on 1024x1024 FP16 on H100
- Test sizes: 256, 1024, 2048, 4096
- atol/rtol: 1e-2 / 1e-3
- FIXME breakdown: 1. enumerate 4 candidate configs; 2. wire `@triton.autotune` decorator; 3. choose key=[M,N,K]; 4. prune unsuitable configs

### E-TRI-05 pid Swizzling
- Topic: tiling-autotuning
- Difficulty: ★★☆
- Concepts exercised: program-id swizzling, GROUP_SIZE_M, L2 cache reuse, wave-front analysis
- Prerequisites: E-TRI-03
- Reference: Triton tutorial 03-matrix-multiplication.py "Pointer Arithmetic" section
- Estimated learner time: 2–4 hours
- Performance target: ≥ 1.15× speedup vs raw row-major pid order on 4096x4096 FP16 on H100
- Test sizes: 1024, 2048, 4096
- atol/rtol: 1e-2 / 1e-3
- FIXME breakdown: 1. compute num_pid_in_group; 2. group_id from pid; 3. first_pid_m within group; 4. final pid_m, pid_n mapping

### E-TRI-06 Persistent Matmul
- Topic: matmul-patterns
- Difficulty: ★★★
- Concepts exercised: persistent kernel pattern, grid sized to SM count, host-side scheduling, `tl.program_id` reuse
- Prerequisites: E-TRI-03, E-TRI-05
- Reference: Triton tutorial 09-persistent-matmul.py
- Estimated learner time: 6–8 hours
- Performance target: ≥ 1.1× speedup vs non-persistent E-TRI-03 on 8192x8192 FP16 on H100
- Test sizes: 2048, 4096, 8192
- atol/rtol: 1e-2 / 1e-3
- FIXME breakdown: 1. compute persistent grid size = SM count; 2. inner loop over tile assignments; 3. K-loop per tile; 4. epilogue store per assignment

### E-TRI-07 FlashAttention Forward
- Topic: attention-reductions
- Difficulty: ★★★
- Concepts exercised: online softmax, block-diagonal masking, KV-block streaming, numerically stable max/sum tracking
- Prerequisites: E-TRI-02, E-TRI-03
- Reference: Dao 2022 FlashAttention paper, Triton tutorial 06-fused-attention.py
- Estimated learner time: 10–14 hours
- Performance target: ≥ 1.5× speedup vs `F.scaled_dot_product_attention` flash-on baseline on seq=2048 head=64 FP16 on H100
- Test sizes: 128, 512, 1024, 2048
- atol/rtol: 1e-2 / 1e-3
- FIXME breakdown: 1. outer Q-block loop; 2. inner KV-block loop; 3. online max tracking; 4. softmax renormalization; 5. accumulate weighted V

### E-TRI-08 FlashAttention Backward
- Topic: attention-reductions
- Difficulty: ★★★
- Concepts exercised: backward pass for online softmax, recomputation strategy, dq/dk/dv kernels
- Prerequisites: E-TRI-07
- Reference: Dao 2022 §4, Triton tutorial 06-fused-attention.py backward block
- Estimated learner time: 12–16 hours
- Performance target: ≥ 1.3× speedup vs SDPA backward on seq=2048 head=64 FP16 on H100
- Test sizes: 128, 512, 1024, 2048
- atol/rtol: 1e-2 / 1e-3
- FIXME breakdown: 1. recompute softmax with saved L, M; 2. dq accumulation kernel; 3. dk accumulation kernel; 4. dv accumulation kernel

### E-TRI-09 Online Softmax
- Topic: attention-reductions
- Difficulty: ★★★
- Concepts exercised: streaming softmax, two-pass to one-pass conversion, numerical stability via running max
- Prerequisites: E-TRI-02
- Reference: Milakov & Gimelshein 2018 "Online normalizer calculation for softmax"
- Estimated learner time: 4–6 hours
- Performance target: ≥ 1.2× speedup vs E-TRI-02 two-pass softmax on row width 8192 FP32 on H100
- Test sizes: 128x1024, 1024x4096, 2048x16384
- atol/rtol: 1e-5 / 1e-5
- FIXME breakdown: 1. running max init; 2. update max on new block; 3. rescale running sum; 4. accumulate new exp sum; 5. final divide

### E-TRI-10 tl.associative_scan Prefix Sum
- Topic: attention-reductions
- Difficulty: ★★☆
- Concepts exercised: `tl.associative_scan`, segment scan, parallel scan primitives
- Prerequisites: E-TRI-01
- Reference: Triton API `tl.associative_scan`, Blelloch 1990
- Estimated learner time: 3–5 hours
- Performance target: ≥ 80% of `torch.cumsum` on N=2^22 FP32 on H100
- Test sizes: 1024, 65536, 1048576, 4194304
- atol/rtol: 1e-3 / 1e-4
- FIXME breakdown: 1. define associative combine op; 2. apply tl.associative_scan over BLOCK_SIZE; 3. cross-block carry kernel; 4. apply carry

### E-TRI-11 TTIR to TTGIR Inspection
- Topic: compiler-internals
- Difficulty: ★★☆
- Concepts exercised: Triton IR dumps, `TRITON_DEBUG=1`, TTIR vs TTGIR vs LLIR, layout encodings
- Prerequisites: E-TRI-03
- Reference: Triton paper §4 (compiler), `triton/python/triton/compiler/code_generator.py`
- Estimated learner time: 3–4 hours
- Performance target: N/A — produce annotated IR dumps for one kernel
- Test sizes: N/A (analysis exercise; deliverable is annotated IR + 2-page writeup)
- atol/rtol: 0 / 0
- FIXME breakdown: 1. compile a kernel with TRITON_DEBUG=1; 2. dump TTIR; 3. dump TTGIR with #blocked encoding; 4. annotate the layout-transform pass

### E-TRI-12 Custom Layout Encoding
- Topic: compiler-internals
- Difficulty: ★★★
- Concepts exercised: TritonGPU dialect, `#shared` encoding, `#mma` encoding, custom layout pass
- Prerequisites: E-TRI-11
- Reference: Triton GitHub `lib/Dialect/TritonGPU/`, MLIR ConvertTritonToTritonGPU pass
- Estimated learner time: 12–16 hours
- Performance target: ≥ 1.05× speedup vs default layout on E-TRI-03 4096x4096 FP16 on H100
- Test sizes: 1024, 2048, 4096
- atol/rtol: 1e-2 / 1e-3
- FIXME breakdown: 1. dump existing #shared encoding; 2. modify swizzle params; 3. recompile with patched layout; 4. measure perf delta

### E-TRI-13 torch.compile Integration
- Topic: ecosystem-production
- Difficulty: ★★☆
- Concepts exercised: `torch.compile` Triton inductor backend, custom kernel registration, fallbacks, recompilation
- Prerequisites: E-TRI-03
- Reference: PyTorch Inductor docs, `torch.utils.cpp_extension` for Triton
- Estimated learner time: 3–5 hours
- Performance target: zero overhead vs hand-launched E-TRI-03 (within 5%) on 1024x1024 FP16 on H100
- Test sizes: 256, 1024, 2048
- atol/rtol: 1e-2 / 1e-3
- FIXME breakdown: 1. register kernel with @torch.library.custom_op; 2. provide meta function; 3. wrap in torch.compile; 4. compare e2e timing

### E-TRI-14 proton Profiling Trace
- Topic: ecosystem-production
- Difficulty: ★★☆
- Concepts exercised: `proton.scope`, `proton.profile`, hierarchical trace export, Chrome trace format
- Prerequisites: E-TRI-03
- Reference: Triton `third_party/proton/`, proton README
- Estimated learner time: 2–4 hours
- Performance target: N/A — produce a Chrome trace JSON for one kernel run
- Test sizes: N/A (deliverable is the trace.json + 1-page analysis)
- atol/rtol: 0 / 0
- FIXME breakdown: 1. wrap kernel call in proton.scope; 2. enable proton.profile; 3. export trace.json; 4. annotate hot ranges

### E-TRI-15 Triton AOT Compilation
- Topic: ecosystem-production
- Difficulty: ★★★
- Concepts exercised: `triton.compile`, ahead-of-time compilation, `.cubin` extraction, host-side launcher integration
- Prerequisites: E-TRI-03, E-TRI-13
- Reference: Triton CLI `triton.tools.aot`, Triton paper §5
- Estimated learner time: 6–8 hours
- Performance target: zero JIT overhead on first call vs eager Triton (within 1%) on 1024x1024 FP16 on H100
- Test sizes: 256, 1024, 2048
- atol/rtol: 1e-2 / 1e-3
- FIXME breakdown: 1. compile kernel AOT with triton.compile; 2. extract .cubin; 3. write C++ launcher with cuLaunchKernel; 4. wire from Python via ctypes
